```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(WVPlots)
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(corrplot)
library(visreg)
library(ggseg)
library(ggsegSchaefer)
library(mgcv)
library(fastDummies)
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(car)
library(purrr)
k=3
load(file="/Users/monaminishio/Downloads/schaefer17_100_3d.RData")
lh_label <- schaefer17_100_3d$ggseg_3d[[1]]['region'][2:51,1:1]
rh_label <- schaefer17_100_3d$ggseg_3d[[2]]['region'][2:51,1:1]
label100 <- rbind(lh_label, rh_label)
schaefer100SA <- read.csv("../derivatives/Schaefer100_sa.csv") 
SA100 <- schaefer100SA$SA
```

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
extract_gam_stats <- function(model, data) {
  rss <- sum(residuals(model)^2)
  tss <- sum((data$df - mean(data$df))^2)
  r_squared <- 1 - (rss / tss)
  p_value <- summary(model)$s.table[1, 4]
  return(list(r_squared = r_squared, p_value = p_value))
}
```

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
process_lme <- function(data, formula_full, n_rows, id, condition) {
  schaefer_H <- data.frame(alcohol=1:n_rows)
  pvalue <- data.frame(alcohol=1:n_rows)
  ctrl <- lmeControl(opt = 'optim')
  for (n in 1:n_rows) {
    col <- colnames(data)[n]
    model <- lmer(as.formula(paste(col, formula_full, sep = " ~ ")), data = data, na.action = na.omit,control = lmerControl(optimizer = "optimx", optCtrl = list(method = "nlminb")))
    aov <- anova(model)
    pvalue$alcohol[n] <- aov$`Pr(>F)`[1]
    schaefer_H$alcohol[n] <- fixef(model)[2]
  }
  correctedpvalue <- data.frame(alcohol=1:n_rows)
  correctedpvalue$alcohol <- p.adjust(pvalue$alcohol, method=c("fdr"))< 0.05
  pvalue$alcohol <- pvalue$alcohol < 0.05
  schaefer_H_extractp <- schaefer_H
  schaefer_H_extractp$alcohol[!pvalue$alcohol] <- NA
  schaefer_H_extractpadj <- schaefer_H
  schaefer_H_extractpadj$alcohol[!correctedpvalue$alcohol] <- NA
  return(list(schaefer_H,schaefer_H_extractpadj,schaefer_H_extractp))
}
```

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
# motion threshold
age_H <- read.csv(paste0('../derivatives/Hurst_schaefer100.csv'))
thresholds <- seq(0.1, 1, by = 0.01)
p_values <- numeric(length(thresholds))
for (i in seq_along(thresholds)) {
  filtered_cross <- age_H[age_H$mean_fd <= thresholds[i], ]
  lme_cross <- lmer(mean_fd ~ Alcohol.No.Alcohol + (1 | sub), data = filtered_cross)
  p_values[i] <- summary(lme_cross)$coefficients['Alcohol.No.AlcoholNoAlcohol', 'Pr(>|t|)']
}
significant_threshold <- max(thresholds[p_values > 0.05])
cat("The mean_fd threshold below which there is no statistically significant difference is:", significant_threshold, "\n")
```

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
age_H <- read.csv(paste0('../derivatives/Hurst_schaefer100.csv'))
age_H <- age_H[age_H$mean_fd<=0.3, ]
age_H <- age_H %>%
  mutate(psess_id = paste(sub, ses, sep = "_"))
age_H <- age_H %>%
  group_by(psess_id) %>%
  mutate(psm_mean_fd = mean(mean_fd, na.rm = TRUE),
         wps_mean_fd = mean_fd - psm_mean_fd,
         psm_num_censored_volumes = mean(num_censored_volumes, na.rm = TRUE),
         wps_num_censored_volumes = num_censored_volumes - psm_num_censored_volumes)
age_H$Alcohol.No.Alcohol <- as.numeric(relevel(factor(age_H$Alcohol.No.Alcohol), ref = "NoAlcohol"))
```

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
# original (motion regressed out) Hurst value
parcel_num = 100
age_H <- dummy_cols(age_H, select_columns = c("sub"))
mean_fd <- age_H$mean_fd
regress_motion <- function(values, mean_fd) {
  df <- data.frame(cross = values, motion = mean_fd)
  colnames(df) <- c('hurst', 'motion')
  lm_res <- lm(hurst ~ motion, data=df)
  residuals(lm_res)             
}
motion_adjusted_data <- sapply(1:100, function(i) regress_motion(age_H[, i], mean_fd))
original_averages <- colMeans(age_H[, 1:100])
adjusted_data <- motion_adjusted_data + (original_averages - colMeans(motion_adjusted_data))
age_H[, 1:100] <- adjusted_data
cross <- age_H[age_H$type == 'cross',]
```

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
# Fig 1B
# Calculate average Hurst exponent across parcels for pre and post conditions
average_pre <- colMeans(cross[cross$Alcohol.No.Alcohol == 1, 1:100])
average_post <- colMeans(cross[cross$Alcohol.No.Alcohol == 2, 1:100])
df_hurst <- data.frame(
  condition = rep(c("Pre", "Post"), c(length(average_pre), length(average_post))),
  hurst = c(average_pre, average_post)
)
# Perform Welch t-test between pre and post conditions (handles unequal sample sizes)
t_test_results <- t.test(average_pre, average_post, var.equal = FALSE)
# Print t-test results
cat(sprintf("t-test results: t = %.2f, p-value = %.4e\n", t_test_results$statistic, t_test_results$p.value))
# Plot the average Hurst values for pre and post conditions
ggplot(df_hurst, aes(x = condition, y = hurst)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = c("black", "white"), alpha = 1) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.1, size = 2, color = "black") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(x = "Condition", y = "Averaged Hurst Exponent", title = "Average Hurst Exponent (Pre vs Post Alcohol)") +
  theme(text = element_text(size = 16))
# Save the plot
ggsave(filename = 'average_hurst_pre_post.eps', device = "pdf", dpi = 500)
```

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
# Fig 1C
# alcohol effect
parcel_num = 100
age_H <- dummy_cols(age_H, select_columns = c("sub"))
cross <- age_H[age_H$type == 'cross',]
results_cross <- process_lme(cross, "Alcohol.No.Alcohol + psm_mean_fd + wps_mean_fd + Gender + (1 + Alcohol.No.Alcohol | sub)", parcel_num, NA, NA) 
df <- results_cross[[1]][1:100, ] 
df <- cbind(df, label100)
ggseg(df, atlas = schaefer17_100, mapping = aes(fill = df),position = "stacked") + scale_fill_gradientn(colours=c("blue", 'white', "red"), name = bquote(alcohol_effect), values = scales::rescale(c(-0.05, 0, 0.05)), limit=c(-0.05, 0.05)) + theme_brain(text.size=16)
ggsave(filename =paste0('Hurst_alcohol_movie.eps'), device = "pdf", dpi = 500)
```

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
# Fig 1D, 2A,B,C,D
# Prepare the list for storing correlation results and p-values
correlation_results <- list()
pvalue_results <- list()
#'NET', 
# Loop through each receptor
for (receptor in c('SA')){#,'GABAa','X5HT1a', 'X5HT1b', 'X5HT2a', 'X5HT4', 'X5HT6', 'X5HTT', 'CB1', 'D1', 'D2', 'DAT',  'H3', 'M1', 'mGluR5', 'MOR','NMDA', 'VAChT', 'A4B2')){
    # Extract data for receptor
    #df <- schaefer100SA[[receptor]]
    #df <- cbind(df, label100)
    #df[['df']] <- scales::rescale(df[['df']], to = c(0, 1))
    ggseg(df, atlas = schaefer17_100, mapping = aes(fill = df), position = "stacked") +
      scale_fill_gradientn(colours = c("white", 'darkblue'), name = bquote(alcohol_effect)) +
      theme_brain(text.size = 16)
    # Save the brain plot
    ggsave(filename = paste0(receptor, '.eps'), device = "pdf", dpi = 500)
    df$SA100 <- SA100
    model <- gam(df ~ s(SA100, k=k), data = df)
    pred <- predict(model, se.fit=T)
    p <- ggplot(data = df, mapping = aes(x = SA100, y = df)) +
      geom_ribbon(aes(ymin=pred$fit-1.96*pred$se.fit,
                       ymax=pred$fit+1.96*pred$se.fit), alpha = 1, fill = 'gray') +
      geom_smooth(method="gam", formula= y~s(x, k=k)) + scale_fill_gradient(low='yellow', high='purple') +
      geom_point(aes(fill = SA100), shape = 21, color = "black", stroke = 0.5, size = 3)  
    print(summary(model))
    ggsave(filename = paste0(receptor, '_SA100.eps'), plot = p, device = "pdf", dpi = 500, height=6)
    # Model fitting and correlation calculation
    SA100 <- schaefer100SA[[receptor]]
    df <- results_cross[[1]][1:100, ]
    df <- cbind(df, label100)
    df$SA100 <- SA100
    df$alcohol <- df[['df']]
    df <- df %>% filter(!is.na(alcohol))
    # Fit GAM model
    #model <- gam(alcohol ~ s(SA100, k = k), data = df)
    #r_squared <- summary(model)$r.sq
    #pvalue <- summary(model)$s.table[1, 4]
    #pred <- predict(model, se.fit = TRUE)
    correlation <- cor.test(df[['df']], SA100, use = "complete.obs")
    coef <- correlation$estimate
    pvalue <- correlation$p.value
    # Perform correlation test to get both correlation and p-value
    #correlation_test <- cor.test(df[['df']], SA100, use = "complete.obs")
    #correlation <- correlation_test$estimate
    #pvalue <- correlation_test$p.value
    #print(c(receptor, pvalue))
    # Store correlation and p-value in the lists
    #correlation_results[[receptor]] <- correlation
    correlation_results[[receptor]] <- coef
    pvalue_results[[receptor]] <- pvalue
    # Print receptor, correlation, and p-value
    cat(sprintf("Receptor: %s, Correlation: %.2f, p-value: %.4e\n", receptor, coef, pvalue))
    # Plot the results with prediction ribbon
    p <- ggplot(data = df, mapping = aes(x = SA100, y = alcohol)) +
      geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = pred$fit - 1.96 * pred$se.fit, ymax = pred$fit + 1.96 * pred$se.fit), alpha = 1, fill = 'gray') +
      geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x) +
      scale_fill_gradient(low = 'lightblue', high = 'darkblue') +
      geom_point(aes(fill = SA100), shape = 21, color = "black", stroke = 0.5, size = 5) +
      theme(legend.position = 'none') + 
      annotate("text", x = Inf, y = Inf, label = paste("r = ", coef, "\n p = ", pvalue), 
               hjust = 1.1, vjust = 1.1, size = 7, color = "black")
    # Save the plotylim(-0.04, 0.04) +
    ggsave(filename = paste0(receptor, '_across_cross_SA100.eps'), plot = p, device = "pdf", dpi = 500)
}
# Convert list to data frame for plotting
correlation_df <- data.frame(
  receptor = names(correlation_results),
  correlation = unlist(correlation_results),
  pvalue = unlist(pvalue_results)
)
# Sort the data frame by correlation values
correlation_df <- correlation_df[order(correlation_df$pvalue), ]
# Plot the sorted correlation results
correlation <- ggplot(correlation_df, aes(x = reorder(receptor, pvalue), y = correlation)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "steelblue") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(x = "Receptor", y = "Correlation", title = "Sorted Correlation of Receptors") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
# Print the sorted correlation and p-values
print(correlation_df)
ggsave(filename = paste0('correlation_grouplevel.eps'), 
       plot = correlation, 
       device = "pdf", 
       dpi = 500, height=6)
```

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
# Fig 2E
# Set up parameters
parcel_num = 100
subjects <- unique(age_H$sub)  # Get unique subjects
output_dir <- "results/"  # Directory to save plots
# Create the output directory if it doesn't exist
if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) {
  dir.create(output_dir)
}
# Initialize a list to store correlation results
correlation_results <- list()
# Function to process and plot for each subject
process_subject <- function(subject) {
  # Subset data for the current subject
  subject_data <- age_H %>%
    filter(sub == subject & type == 'cross')
  # Run the LME model
  results_cross <- process_lme(subject_data,  "Alcohol.No.Alcohol + psm_mean_fd + wps_mean_fd +  (1 | psess_id)",parcel_num, NA, NA) 
  # Prepare the dataframe for plottingAlcohol.No.Alcohol*psm_mean_fd + Alcohol.No.Alcohol*wps_mean_fd +
  df <- results_cross[[1]][1:100, ] 
  #print(min(df[['df']]))
  #print(max(df[['df']]))
  df <- cbind(df, label100)  # Assuming label100 is defined in the environment
  
  # Create and save the main plot
  p <- ggseg(df, atlas = schaefer17_100, mapping = aes(fill = df),
             position = "stacked") +
    scale_fill_gradientn(colours = c("blue", 'white', "red"), 
                         name = bquote(alcohol_effect), 
                         values = scales::rescale(c(-0.5, -0.05, 0, 0.05, 0.5)), 
                         limits = c(-0.5, 0.5)) +
    theme_brain(text.size = 16) +
    ggtitle(paste("Subject", subject))
  ggsave(filename = paste0(output_dir, 'Hurst_alcohol_cross_subject_', subject, '.eps'), 
         plot = p, 
         device = "pdf", 
         dpi = 500)
  # Loop over receptors to compute and save correlation coefficients
  subject_correlations <- list() #'A4B2',  'NET',, 'SA'
  for (receptor in c('X5HT1a', 'X5HT1b', 'X5HT2a', 'X5HT4', 'X5HT6', 'X5HTT',  'CB1', 'D1', 'D2', 'DAT', 'GABAa', 'H3', 'M1', 'mGluR5', 'MOR', 'NMDA', 'VAChT', 'A4B2')) {
    if (receptor %in% names(schaefer100SA)) {
      SA100 <- schaefer100SA[[receptor]]
      # Ensure SA100 has the same length as df
      if (length(SA100) == nrow(df)) {
        correlation <- cor.test(df[['df']], SA100, use = "complete.obs")
        coef <- correlation$estimate
        pvalue <- correlation$p.value
        #model <- gam(df ~ s(SA100, k = k), data = df)
        #r_squared <- summary(model)$r.sq
        #pvalue <- summary(model)$s.table[1, 4]
        
        subject_correlations[[receptor]] <- data.frame(
          Subject = as.factor(subject),  # Ensure Subject is a factor
          Receptor = receptor,
          Correlation = coef,
          Pvalue = pvalue
        )
      } else {
        message("Mismatch in lengths for receptor ", receptor, " for subject ", subject)
      }
    } else {
      message("Receptor ", receptor, " not found in schaefer100SA for subject ", subject)
    }
  }
  do.call(rbind, subject_correlations)
}
correlation_results <- map_dfr(subjects, process_subject)
t_test_results <- correlation_results %>%
  group_by(Receptor) %>%
  summarise(p_value = t.test(Correlation, mu = 0)$p.value)
correlation_results <- correlation_results %>%
  left_join(t_test_results, by = "Receptor") %>%
  mutate(Receptor = factor(Receptor, levels = t_test_results$Receptor))
# Create the combined boxplot and scatterplot
combined_plot <- ggplot(correlation_results, aes(x = reorder(Receptor, p_value), y = Correlation)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_jitter(aes(color = Subject), width = 0.2, size = 1.5, alpha = 0.6) +  # Scatter plot for each subject
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(title = "Correlation of Receptors across Subjects", x = "Receptor (sorted by p-value)", y = "Correlation") +
  scale_color_viridis_d() 
# Display the p-values for each receptor
print(t_test_results)
# Save the combined plot
ggsave(filename = paste0('correlation_coefficients_combined_plot.eps'), 
       plot = combined_plot, 
       device = "pdf", 
       dpi = 500, height=6)
```
